content.problemStatement:
For a (000-indexed) binary string SSS of length NNN, define f(S)=f(S) = f(S)= the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j) (i,j) such that 0‚Č§i<j<N0 \leq i \lt j \lt N0‚Č§i<j<N and Si=0,Sj=1S_i = 0, S_j = 1Si‚Äč=0,Sj‚Äč=1.

Given a binary string SSS of size NNN, you can perform the following operation **exactly once**:


Choose an integer KKK such that 1<K‚Č§N1 \lt K \le N1<K‚Č§N and N‚Äämod‚ÄäK=0N \bmod K = 0NmodK=0, and divide the string into continuous substrings of size KKK, i.e. you will have a list of NK\frac{N}{K}KN‚Äč substrings S[0,K‚ąí1],S[K,2‚čÖK‚ąí1],‚Ä¶,S[N‚ąíK,N‚ąí1]S[0, K-1], S[K, 2 \cdot K-1], \ldots, S[N - K, N-1]S[0,K‚ąí1],S[K,2‚čÖK‚ąí1],‚Ä¶,S[N‚ąíK,N‚ąí1].

Here, S[L,R]S[L, R]S[L,R] denotes the string formed by concatenating the characters SL,SL+1,SL+2,‚Ä¶,SRS_L, S_{L + 1}, S_{L + 2}, \ldots, S_RSL‚Äč,SL+1‚Äč,SL+2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SR‚Äč in this order.


Rearrange the list of NK\frac{N}{K}KN‚Äč substrings in some order, and concatenate the substrings to form the new string SSS.
Find the **maximum** possible value of f(S)f(S)f(S) after this operation has been performed.

You will be given the string SSS in encoded format.

We will provide you with an array AAA of size M=‚ĆąN30‚ĆČM = \left\lceil \frac{N}{30} \right\rceilM=‚Ćą30N‚Äč‚ĆČ. The original character SiS_iSi‚Äč can be obtained as

f(A[  ‚Ćäi30‚Ćč  ],i‚Äämod‚Ää30)f\left(A\left[\ \ \left\lfloor \frac{i}{30} \right\rfloor\ \ \right], i \bmod 30\right)f(A[  ‚Ćä30i‚Äč‚Ćč  ],imod30)where f(x,y)f(x, y)f(x,y) represents the yyy-th bit of xxx, and ‚Ćäx‚Ćč\left\lfloor x \right\rfloor‚Ćäx‚Ćč is the largest integer that doesn't exceed xxx.

Consider array AAA and the string SSS to be 000-indexed.

See the samples below for more clarity on the input format.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line contains two integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the length of string SSS, and the length of the encoded array AAA, respectively.
The second line contains MMM space-separated integers A0,A1,‚Ä¶,AM‚ąí1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{M-1}A0‚Äč,A1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AM‚ąí1‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the encoded array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum value of f(S)f(S)f(S) after one operation.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all test cases does not exceed 4‚čÖ1074 \cdot 10^74‚čÖ107.
**Subtask 1 (40 points):** The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The actual string SSS is 1011\text{1011}1011.
We can choose K=4K = 4K=4, and not affect the string (since only 111 substring is formed, we have to concatenate it back in this order). This obtains f(S)=2f(S) = 2f(S)=2, which is optimal.
Test case 222: The actual string is 1100\text{1100}1100.
We can choose K=2K = 2K=2, divide the string into 2 substrings 11\text{11}11 and 00\text{00}00, then concatenate them back in reverse order to get 0011\text{0011}0011. This obtains f(S)=4f(S) = 4f(S)=4, which is optimal.
Test case 333: The encoded string SSS is
100000000011101000111101000000100111011000011110000000\text{100000000011101000111101000000100111011000011110000000}100000000011101000111101000000100111011000011110000000
